{"key_event":"COVID emergency governance amid polarization","reasoning":"Civil society remained strong through mass protest, ballot measures, litigation, online mobilization, and a modest union revival, but polarization, misinformation, inequality, and institutional distrust made that power more fragmented rather than clearly stronger. State power stayed extremely high after pandemic emergency authority, fiscal transfers, prosecutions, and industrial-policy capacity, yet judicial constraints, federal-state conflict, debt-ceiling brinkmanship, and election-legitimacy disputes slightly weakened coherent national authority.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":7.9,"state_change":-0.1,"state_power":9.2}
